# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @products = Product.all
    
    if params[:search].present?
      search_params = params[:search]
      
      # Safe column name allowlist
      allowed_columns = %w[name description category]
      allowed_operators = %w[= != < > <= >= LIKE]
      
      # Filter by name
      if search_params[:name].present?
        @products = @products.where("name LIKE ?", "%#{search_params[:name]}%")
      end
      
      # Filter by category
      if search_params[:category].present?
        @products = @products.where(category: search_params[:category])
      end
      
      # Filter by price range
      if search_params[:min_price].present? || search_params[:max_price].present?
        price_conditions = []
        price_values = []
        
        if search_params[:min_price].present?
          price_conditions << "price >= ?"
          price_values << search_params[:min_price]
        end
        
        if search_params[:max_price].present?
          price_conditions << "price <= ?"
          price_values << search_params[:max_price]
        end
        
        @products = @products.where(price_conditions.join(" AND "), *price_values)
      end
    end
    
    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(20)
  end
end
